skill.name: viral-captions-and-ctas
skill.description: Create platform-ready captions, on-screen text, hashtags, CTAs, and pinned comments for short-form videos on Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.
skill.summary: Write short-form video captions and CTAs that support saves, sends, search, and clean engagement.
skill.author: vyralcontent

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, inline file references, labels, hashtags, and social-media guidance rather than executable code. No command execution, cryptographic code, screen upload routine, reconnaissance behavior, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found. The only confirmed issue is a low-risk external promotional/image URL in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Promotional URL in Markdown
low_findings.description: SKILL.md embeds an external image and link in Markdown. This is not executable code and does not exfiltrate secrets, but rendered marketplace pages may request third-party content or send users to an external commercial site.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is directly visible in Markdown on the cited line. It is low risk because there is no code path, credential access, or automatic data submission.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are inline Markdown references and fenced text templates. They describe caption and CTA formats; they do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown prose, inline file names, or fenced examples. No executable script files or command invocation syntax are present.

low_findings.title: Text Labels Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary words, headings, and template labels such as description and hashtags. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows plain copywriting templates and platform guidance. There is no code, cryptographic library, or weak algorithm call to execute.

low_findings.title: Screenshot Language Misclassified as Screen Upload
low_findings.description: The screen capture upload finding is documentation about burned-in captions surviving screenshots, downloads, and re-uploads. It does not contain any upload mechanism or file access logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is explanatory prose about social video behavior. There is no API call, network endpoint, filesystem access, or upload workflow.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Editorial Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to content-planning text, platform tables, or editor checklists. They do not inspect the host system, scan networks, enumerate files, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are user-facing instructions about analytics, editing, platform metadata, and company wording. No host or network reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.user_title: Write Better Short-Form Captions and CTAs
content.value_statement: Short-form videos often lose reach because captions, hashtags, and CTAs send weak signals. This skill turns a video brief into platform-ready copy that supports saves, sends, search, and clean engagement.
actual_capabilities: Drafts captions for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts with platform-specific visible cutoffs.
Selects one primary CTA based on the video format, audience goal, and desired viewer action.
Creates small, niche hashtag sets and explains when hashtags add little value.
Specs on-screen text layouts, burned-in captions, safe zones, and readable mobile styling.
Writes pinned comment options for objection handling, links, product lists, and second hooks.
Checks draft copy for engagement-bait patterns and rewrites risky phrasing.
limitations: It improves copy quality but cannot guarantee virality or reach.
It does not access live platform analytics unless the user provides data.
It cannot verify current promotions, pricing, or external site claims.
It is focused on short-form social video, not long-form scripts or full campaign strategy.

use_cases.title: Launch a Creator Video
use_cases.description: Turn a rough video idea into a caption, CTA, hashtags, and pinned comment before posting.

use_cases.title: Repair Weak Brand Copy
use_cases.description: Replace vague engagement asks with save, send, or comment prompts that match the video format.

use_cases.title: Adapt One Video Across Platforms
use_cases.description: Rewrite the same concept for Reels, TikTok, and Shorts with native cutoffs and hashtag conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Caption Fast
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a basic video brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a caption for my TikTok about [topic]. The audience is [audience], and the goal is [save, send, comment, or watch full]. Include 3 to 5 hashtags and one pinned comment.

prompt_templates.title: Fix My CTA
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing call to action.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CTA for engagement bait: [CTA]. Keep the same intent, then rewrite it for saves, sends, and real comments.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Copy Pack
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare all text surfaces for one short-form video.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the full copy layer for a [platform] video about [topic]. Include the visible caption line, body caption, hashtags, on-screen text spec, CTA timing, and pinned comment.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Across Platforms
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for native platform rewrites.
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this video copy for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts: [draft]. Preserve the core idea, but rewrite cutoffs, keywords, hashtags, CTA placement, and pinned comment strategy for each platform.

output_examples.input: A 45-second Reel about stopping retinol peeling for sensitive skin.
output: Visible caption: The reason your retinol is peeling is not the retinol.
CTA: Send this to your friend who keeps blaming the brand.
Pinned comment: Products in order, plus the day 14 update.

output_examples.input: A TikTok list of three beginner sourdough mistakes.
output: Visible caption: Three sourdough mistakes that look harmless at first.
CTA: Save this before your next starter feed.
Hashtags: Use three to five niche baking tags, not broad viral tags.

output_examples.input: A YouTube Short teasing a full camera review.
output: Title: The budget camera mistake beginners keep making.
CTA: Full breakdown is on the channel with the test shots.
Pinned comment: Link the long-form review and name the exact comparison.

best_practices: Choose one primary viewer action before writing the caption.
Place the main keyword early in the visible caption and reinforce it in on-screen text.
Use pinned comments for missing context, product lists, objections, or a second hook.
anti_patterns: Do not stack like, save, share, follow, and comment asks in one video.
Do not use generic hashtag walls or broad filler tags such as viral and fyp.
Do not promise a free resource and send users to an unrelated sales page.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee a viral video?
faq.answer: No. It improves caption and CTA choices using common performance patterns, but results depend on the video, audience, timing, and platform.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts, with separate guidance for captions, hashtags, CTAs, and pinned comments.

faq.question: Can it rewrite existing copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can diagnose weak captions or bait-shaped CTAs and rewrite them for saves, sends, comments, or watch-through.

faq.question: Does it need analytics data?
faq.answer: No. It can work from a brief, but retention or drop-off data helps place CTAs more precisely.

faq.question: How many hashtags will it recommend?
faq.answer: It usually recommends three to five niche tags and avoids broad filler tags that do not improve discovery.

faq.question: Can it create on-screen text instructions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can specify headline text, burned-in caption rhythm, font weight, safe zones, contrast, and keyword alignment.
